source_text: For text, use 10 to 12 point font size. Times New Roman 12-point is the preferred font style and size for official correspondence, but Courier New may be used for informal correspondence.
plain_language_rule: Standard letter body text must use 10 to 12 point font size. Times New Roman 12-point is the preferred font for official correspondence. Courier New may be used for informal correspondence.
renderer_impact: Font selection and sizing throughout document body text; renderer must support Times New Roman 12pt as the default for official correspondence, with Courier New as an acceptable alternative for informal correspondence. Font size range 10–12pt is permitted.
notes: The manual specifies 10–12pt font for body text, with Times New Roman 12pt preferred for official correspondence and Courier New acceptable for informal use. There is a surface-level tension between 7-2.1's prohibition on proportional spacing and the explicit approval of Times New Roman (a proportional font). The proportional-spacing prohibition likely refers to character-spacing/kerning adjustments, not proportional font families. This ambiguity is preserved as-is from the manual. Header elements may use different sizes per their specific requirements.
